content.content: The file /tmp/klaus-sessions/reel-life/20260328-1646-5460/nix/module.nix has been updated successfully.

lines: +#   This module runs reel-life as a sandboxed systemd service with DynamicUser,
+#   strict filesystem protection, capability dropping, syscall filtering, and
+#   Expected score: ~2.0 (on a 0–10 scale where lower is more secure).
+#   for localhost (Sonarr) access. Since IPAddressAllow works with IPs, not DNS
+#   names, external APIs (api.anthropic.com, chat.googleapis.com) require either:
+#     1. Adding their IP ranges to `allowedHosts` (IPs change over time), or
+#     2. Using nftables for DNS-aware filtering (recommended for production):
+#           type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept;
+#           meta skuid "reel-life" tcp dport { 80, 443 } ip daddr {
+#   Note: nftables rules can reference DNS names resolved at rule-load time.
+#   For dynamic IPs, consider a systemd timer that periodically re-resolves
description = "Files containing environment variables (for secrets like API keys)";
+        When enabled, restricts network access to allowedHosts only via
+        systemd's IPAddressAllow/IPAddressDeny. Note: external API access
+        (Anthropic, Google Chat) requires adding their IP ranges to
+        allowedHosts, or using nftables for DNS-aware filtering.
+        IP addresses the service is allowed to connect to when
+        restrictNetwork is enabled. Sonarr (localhost) is always allowed
+        via the defaults. Add resolved IPs for api.anthropic.com and
+        # Run as an ephemeral system user
+        # Filesystem: strict read-only root, no home, private /tmp and /dev
+        # Kernel: block access to tunables, modules, logs, cgroups, clock, hostname
+        # Syscall filtering: allow standard service calls, deny dangerous ones
+        # Security: lock down personality, namespaces, realtime, SUID/SGID
# Secrets via environment files (e.g. agenix paths)
+        # Network allowlist: only permit traffic to specified IPs

input.description: Stage changes and show diff stats

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Harden NixOS systemd service and add network allowlisting

Extend the NixOS module with comprehensive systemd sandboxing:
- Full kernel protection (modules, logs, clock, hostname)
- Drop all capabilities, restrict syscalls to @system-service
- Lock personality, namespaces, address families (IPv4/IPv6/Unix only)
- Resource limits (256M memory, 50% CPU, 32 tasks)
- Optional network allowlist via restrictNetwork/allowedHosts options
  using systemd IPAddressAllow/IPAddressDeny
- Documentation for nftables-based DNS-aware filtering alternative

Expected systemd-analyze security score: ~2.0/10.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Harden NixOS systemd service and add network allowlisting" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Extends the NixOS module's systemd service with comprehensive sandboxing: full kernel protection, capability dropping, syscall filtering (`@system-service` allowlist), namespace/address-family restrictions, and resource limits (256M/50% CPU/32 tasks)
- Adds `restrictNetwork` and `allowedHosts` options for IP-based network allowlisting via systemd's `IPAddressAllow`/`IPAddressDeny`
- Documents nftables-based alternative for DNS-aware filtering of external APIs (Anthropic, Google Chat) whose IPs change dynamically

## Test plan
- [x] `nix flake check` passes — module evaluates correctly
- [x] `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` still pass
- [ ] Deploy to a NixOS host and verify `systemd-analyze security reel-life` scores ~2.0
- [ ] Verify service starts and can reach Sonarr on localhost
- [ ] Test `restrictNetwork = true` blocks unexpected outbound connections
- [ ] Test adding external API IPs to `allowedHosts` restores Anthropic/Google access

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Run: 20260328-1646-5460
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/reel-life/pull/4

Here's what was done:

**Hardening added** (extending existing settings, no duplication):
- Kernel protection: `ProtectKernelModules`, `ProtectKernelLogs`, `ProtectClock`, `ProtectHostname`
- Capabilities: `CapabilityBoundingSet = ""`, `AmbientCapabilities = ""`
- Syscall filtering: `@system-service` allowlist with deny groups for mount, module, reboot, swap, raw-io, clock, debug, obsolete
- `SystemCallArchitectures = "native"`, `LockPersonality`, `RestrictRealtime`, `RestrictNamespaces`
- `RestrictAddressFamilies` limited to IPv4, IPv6, Unix
- Resource limits: 256M memory, 50% CPU, 32 tasks
- `ReadWritePaths = []` (stateless service)

**Network allowlisting** via two new options:
- `restrictNetwork` (bool, default false) — enables `IPAddressDeny = "any"`
- `allowedHosts` (list of IPs, default `["127.0.0.1" "::1"]`) — sets `IPAddressAllow`

**Documentation** at the top of the module explains the hardening, how to test with `systemd-analyze security`, and how to use nftables for DNS-aware filtering of external APIs.

result:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/reel-life/pull/4

Here's what was done:

**Hardening added** (extending existing settings, no duplication):
- Kernel protection: `ProtectKernelModules`, `ProtectKernelLogs`, `ProtectClock`, `ProtectHostname`
- Capabilities: `CapabilityBoundingSet = ""`, `AmbientCapabilities = ""`
- Syscall filtering: `@system-service` allowlist with deny groups for mount, module, reboot, swap, raw-io, clock, debug, obsolete
- `SystemCallArchitectures = "native"`, `LockPersonality`, `RestrictRealtime`, `RestrictNamespaces`
- `RestrictAddressFamilies` limited to IPv4, IPv6, Unix
- Resource limits: 256M memory, 50% CPU, 32 tasks
- `ReadWritePaths = []` (stateless service)

**Network allowlisting** via two new options:
- `restrictNetwork` (bool, default false) — enables `IPAddressDeny = "any"`
- `allowedHosts` (list of IPs, default `["127.0.0.1" "::1"]`) — sets `IPAddressAllow`

**Documentation** at the top of the module explains the hardening, how to test with `systemd-analyze security`, and how to use nftables for DNS-aware filtering of external APIs.
